jobs.description: Creative Strategist Purpose We are looking for the dialed Creative Strategists to join our 8 - figure eCommerce company. This is a growing team that is scaling hard, and we want people who want to be part of something that is already winning. The Creative Strategist owns the thinking behind our ads turning customer insights, market research, and data into concepts that drive real results. Your job is to know why our customers buy, communicate that in a way that stops the scroll, and brief the team to bring it to life. You'll be assigned to a specific product or collection, working alongside a team of other Creative Strategists. Every concept you develop is yours from idea through to handoff you own it start to finish. What You'll Own Onboarding: Deep-dive into your assigned product — reviews, competitors, customer psychology Study the customer avatar and understand the core buyer Get across our creative systems, tools, and testing frameworks Ongoing: Develop 5-10 creative concepts per week grounded in customer psychology and data Write clear briefs for UGC creators, video editors, and graphic designers Manage and communicate directly with your assigned editor, designer, and UGC creators Write or select proven Facebook ad copy for every concept you develop Review content against briefs and provide structured feedback before delivery Own every concept from development through to handoff to the media buyer Analyse performance metrics (hook rate, hold rate, CTR, CVR) weekly Document all learnings into the Creative Road Map — closing the loop is non-negotiable Apply learnings directly into your next round of concepts Write SOPs and document processes via Loom or written documentation Develop creatives across Facebook, YouTube, and other paid platforms How We Work We don't just test and move on. Every creative gets reviewed, every learning gets documented, and every insight feeds the next concept. If you're not learning and improving every cycle, you're not doing the job. Tools We Use ClickUp — project management and creative pipeline Discord — daily team communication Frame.io — creative review and feedback Shopify — our storefronts And other research tools are available What We're Looking For 1.5+ years in performance creative, paid social, or creative strategy Experience briefing and directing UGC creators, video editors, and graphic designers Comfortable reading ad metrics and using data to drive creative decisions Experience writing direct response ad copy for Facebook or similar platforms eCommerce background a strong plus Organised, accountable, and disciplined about closing the loop Coachable, curious, and hungry to grow This Is NOT For You If: You think creative is about aesthetics, not performance You wait to be told what to test You skip documentation and ignore the feedback loop You can't handle direct feedback or a fast-moving environment Hours We are based in Australia, a similar time zone is a bonus, but not a requirement. approximately 40 hours per week. Flexibility available based on performance. Compensation Competitive base rate plus monthly performance bonuses tied to your top-spending and top-performing creatives. The better your output, the higher your earning potential. What You Get Access To 1-on-1 calls with our internal creative team A cross-creative team environment with other high-level strategists Free access to premium courses and resources A team that invests in your professional and personal growth Great opportunity to grow as a world-class marketer and be a part of 9-Figure ecom company Growth Potential Performance drives everything here. Strong results open the door to Senior Strategist and Creative Lead roles as we grow. Apply with your resume and a brief breakdown of a creative concept you've built and what results it drove. Use the subject title: Killer Strategist (Your Name) Note: You will be ignored if you don't apply with the mentioned subject line and portfolio.

jobs.description: 1. Project Overview We are a premium D2C outdoor furniture brand targeting high-income North American homeowners. We are looking for a world-class UI/UX designer to create a high-end Homepage and a Product Detail Page (PDP) Template in Figma. Our goal is to achieve RH.com’s (Restoration Hardware) luxury minimalist aesthetics combined with 7thavenue.co’s functional clarity. 2. The Design Challenge We don't just sell furniture; we sell precision engineering and tactile luxury. Aesthetic Goal: Sophisticated typography, massive "breathing room" (negative space), and a gallery-like minimalist feel. Tactile Storytelling: We need a "Macro-level" visual presentation. The layout must highlight the textures of our high-density wicker weaves and premium waterproof cushion fabrics, allowing customers to virtually "feel" the quality through the screen. 3. Product Scope & Logic Each of our furniture sets (ranging from 3-piece to 11-piece "Grand Estate" configurations) has its own dedicated SKU link. No complex configurator is needed. Instead, we need a perfectly architected PDP template that can showcase large modular sets with premium impact and clarity. Color Management: Each set comes with 6-7 premium cushion color options. The UI must display these swatches elegantly without cluttering the minimalist aesthetic. 4. Mandatory Deliverables We are looking for a Scalable Design System, not just one-off page designs. Mobile Design (375px): Optimized for touch, ensuring macro-details remain striking on small screens. Desktop Design (1440px & 1920px): Utilizing wide-screen real estate for cinematic storytelling. Figma Design System: Full component library (Auto-layout), typography scales, and custom iconography/symbols. 5. What We Provide (See Attachments) 4K CGI Renders: Lifestyle scenes and macro-detail crops. Brand Assets: List of technical selling points and modular set combinations. Color Palette: References for the 6-7 cushion colors. 6. Budget & Expertise Our budget is flexible for the right expert who has a proven track record in luxury D2C furniture or high-end home decor. Please bid your true value based on the dual-device (375px/1440px/1920px) requirements and the depth of the Design System. 7. Screening Questions (Required to Answer) Portfolio Link: Please provide links to at least one Furniture or Luxury Home project you have designed. Texture Presentation: How do you ensure high-density textures (like wicker/fabric) remain visually impactful on a 375px mobile screen without compromising speed? Visual Balance: With 7 cushion color options, how do you design the selection UI to remain "minimalist" and avoid a cluttered "catalog" look? RH Style: How do you define "Premium Negative Space," and how will you apply it to our 11-piece large sets?
jobs.title: UI/UX Designer for Luxury Outdoor Furniture - Shopify Homepage & PDP Template

jobs.description: We are an e-commerce marketplace looking to expand our remote operations team. We are looking for VAs that can help us with ongoing work covering data entry, copywriting, general internet-based operational tasks and more. This is not a one-off gig. We're looking for people who can grow with us. The right hires will have the opportunity to move into full-time roles taking on more varied and interesting work across the organization — sourcing, sales support, logistics, copywriting, file management and other areas depending on where you show strength and interest. About the work: The day-to-day will be varied. Examples of what you'll be doing initially: - Data entry into spreadsheets and our internal systems (product listings, inventory, supplier details, shipping trackers) - Copywriting — product descriptions, email replies, listing titles, internal documentation - Online research — finding suppliers/customers, comparing prices, sourcing information, building contact lists, doing cold outreach - Purchasing items online on our behalf using a company credit card we'll issue you - Coordinating with vendors, freelancers, and platforms via email - General operational tasks that keep the business running smoothly This work is the backbone of our operation. Done well, it directly enables everything else we do, from sourcing new inventory to delivering a great experience to our customers. As you prove yourself, the scope can expand significantly. Who we're looking for: The quality that matters most to us is high agency. When you hit a problem, your instinct is to solve it. You make sensible judgment calls, flag what needs flagging, and try to solve problems within your control instead of waiting for instructions. We want people we can hand a goal to and trust to figure out the right approach. You'll also be working with sensitive access — company accounts, supplier details, and a company credit card for purchases — so a strong track record in roles with similar responsibilities is essential. Required: - 2+ years of remote work experience, ideally as a VA or in a similar operations role - Excellent written English — clear, professional, low-error - Fast learner and highly adaptable - High trust and high agency - Comfortable with Google Workspace (Sheets, Docs, Gmail, Drive) - Reliable internet and a quiet workspace - Available to work on US business hours Nice to have: - Experience purchasing on behalf of an employer (references welcome) - Experience working within company internal systems (provide examples) - Familiarity with e-commerce platforms (Shopify, eBay) - Copywriting samples you can share - Experience with tools like Slack, Linear, Attio (or other CRM)

jobs.description: Job Overview: We are a commercial fitness equipment ecommerce company operating Fitness Superstore and French Fitness. We sell new French Fitness equipment, remanufactured commercial fitness equipment, accessories, installation services, and related products. We are looking for a senior Product Catalog & Category Manager to help own the commercial truth of our product catalog. This is not a basic product upload role. We already have team members who can update Shopify, product pages, and spreadsheets. We need someone who can review a messy product/category structure, identify what should be consolidated, cleaned, prioritized, improved, or escalated, and help create a reliable source of truth across Shopify, product data, SKUs, specs, options, bundles, accessories, and PDPs. Our highest-priority brand is French Fitness, our house brand. We need better structure around product families, SKUs, options, accessories, bundles, content, product-page accuracy, related products, upsells, and category priorities. You must be comfortable working with messy real-world product data and turning it into clear decisions. What You Will Work On: - Review product families, SKUs, variants, duplicate products, options, and add-ons. - Help build a clean master product/category structure. - Identify hero products and high-priority PDPs. - Review product specs, warranty, processing time, shipping status, accessories, upsells, and related products. - Help organize products by brand, category, condition, supplier/factory, margin/commercial priority, and product family. - Work with Shopify product exports, Google Sheets, metafields, metaobjects, and product data. - Coordinate with internal product, Shopify, content, warehouse/inventory, and operations team members. - Create clear action plans: what to update, what to consolidate, what to hide, what to leave alone, and what needs management approval. - Help prevent bad imports or unapproved changes from being pushed live. - Help us decide which products deserve better PDPs, better images/video, bundles, accessories, comparison content, or cleanup. Required Experience: - Shopify product catalog management. - Product data cleanup and SKU/category organization. - Ecommerce category management or ecommerce merchandising. - Strong spreadsheet skills. - Ability to work with product exports and structured product data. - Experience with variants, bundles, metafields, product specs, and PDP content. - Excellent attention to detail. - Ability to explain tradeoffs clearly. - Ability to separate data entry from product/category decision-making. Strong Bonus Experience: - Fitness equipment, sporting goods, tools, industrial equipment, furniture, automotive, appliances, medical equipment, or another spec-heavy product category. - Shopify Plus. - Shopify metafields and metaobjects. - Google Merchant Center / shopping feed awareness. - Product lifecycle management. - Category merchandising. - Bundle/upsell/accessory mapping. - Product information management / PIM experience. - Working with remote ecommerce, product, warehouse, and operations teams. What This Role Is Not: - Not just product upload. - Not just copywriting. - Not just SEO. - Not just Shopify development. - Not a generic VA role. - Not someone who blindly imports product data without review. First Project / Paid Test: For the paid test, we will give you a small sample of product data and ask you to: 1. Identify duplicate or confusing SKUs. 2. Recommend the master product structure. 3. Flag missing specs, conflicting specs, or risky assumptions. 4. Suggest PDP improvements. 5. Recommend related products, upsells, accessories, and category priority. 6. Identify what should be updated, merged, hidden, left alone, or escalated. 7. Provide a simple action plan for the internal team. Proposal Instructions: Please start your proposal with: “I can own the product catalog truth.” Then answer the 5 screening questions below. Expected Workload: Initial trial: 10–20 hours. If the trial goes well: 10–25 hours/week. We are looking for a long-term contractor who can help us improve the structure and accuracy of our product catalog over time.

jobs.description: Old Dog New Treats (ODNT) is a Colorado-based functional soft treat brand for senior dogs. Our three lab-tested formulas — Brain Boost, Mobility Boost, and Tender Heart — are built on real science and sold through our Shopify DTC store. ODNT was founded after years of personal research into the best possible care for an aging dog. Our founder left a career in policy law to build something that actually helps senior dog owners navigate this chapter with confidence. Every ingredient is vetted. Every claim is grounded. The brand voice is warm, emotionally resonant, and science-backed, never corporate. We've sold out at Denver events, built a newsletter audience (The Distinguished Snout), and established a growing Shopify presence with bundles, review flows, and Klaviyo email sequences. We're now ready to scale. The Role We're looking for a marketing strategist to partner with a founder-led brand and help us grow meaningfully, not just boost vanity metrics. You'll have a clear point of view on the pet market, understand DTC brand-building, and know how to move a small brand from "hidden gem" to "name people know." This is a collaborative, ongoing relationship. We want someone who understands that senior dog owners are a passionate, underserved audience — and can help us reach them and convert them. Core Work Areas 1. Customer & Audience Analysis • Define and document our ideal customer profile (ICP) — who buys, why, and what drives repeat purchase • Analyze existing customer data, reviews, and social engagement to surface behavioral patterns • Identify where our audience lives online and how to reach them authentically • Competitive landscape review — where does ODNT stand, and what's the whitespace? 2. Website & Shopify Conversion Strategy • Audit current Shopify store for conversion gaps and user experience issues • Develop recommendations for product page optimization, bundle strategy, and checkout improvements • Strategy for driving organic and paid traffic to the store • Guidance on SEO fundamentals for our product category and content 3. Social Media Strategy & Growth • Instagram and Facebook growth strategy — organic content pillars, posting cadence, engagement tactics • Reels / video content strategy tailored to the senior dog audience • Community-building guidance: how to grow a loyal audience, not just followers • Guidance on UGC strategy, influencer/ambassador programs, and social proof 4. Email Marketing Optimization • Review and optimize our existing Klaviyo flow structure (welcome, abandoned cart, post-purchase, win-back) • Newsletter strategy for The Distinguished Snout — audience growth and engagement • Segmentation and personalization recommendations 5. Paid Advertising Guidance • Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ads strategy — audience targeting, creative direction, budget allocation for a small brand • Guidance on Reddit ads for niche pet communities • Help defining realistic ROAS targets and performance benchmarks 6. Overall Growth Roadmap • 30/60/90 day priorities and quick wins • Channel prioritization — where should a lean brand focus first? • Ongoing monthly strategy check-ins and performance reviews Requirements — Please Read Before Applying Must-Have Experience • Proven pet brand marketing experience (not general wellness or human supplements) • DTC / e-commerce background with measurable growth results • Shopify platform familiarity • Klaviyo or comparable ESP experience • Meta Ads management and strategy experience • Direct experience working with small, founder-led brands Please Do NOT Apply If: • Your background is primarily general wellness, beauty, or human supplements • You've only worked with large CPG or enterprise retail brands • You've only worked agency-side with no direct brand ownership experience We've worked hard to build a brand with a specific voice and science-backed positioning. We need someone who understands the pet space, the emotional relationship people have with their dogs, and what it takes to build trust in this category. Rate Guidance We're open to hourly and project-based arrangements. Below are our benchmarks by experience level. Please include your rate and preferred engagement type in your proposal. Emerging Specialist (3–5 yrs pet/DTC experience): $45–$75/hr Mid-Level Strategist (5–8 yrs, strong portfolio): $75–$120/hr Senior Consultant (proven pet brand growth results): $120–$175/hr Estimated Project Scopes Below are typical engagement sizes and investment ranges based on market rates for experienced pet/DTC specialists: Engagement Scope Est. Hours Est. Investment Brand & Customer Audit ICP definition, customer journey map, competitive review, channel audit 8–12 hrs $600–$1,400 Shopify Conversion Audit Store UX review, product page optimization brief, SEO baseline 5–8 hrs $375–$960 Social Media Strategy Platform strategy, content pillars, 90-day calendar framework, growth roadmap 8–14 hrs $600–$1,680 Klaviyo / Email Audit + Strategy Flow review, segmentation recommendations, newsletter growth plan 4–8 hrs $300–$960 Meta Ads Strategy Audience targeting, creative brief, campaign structure, KPI benchmarks 6–10 hrs $450–$1,200 Full Growth Roadmap All of the above + 30/60/90 day plan + channel prioritization, 30–50 hrs $2,250–$6,000 Ongoing Monthly Retainer Strategy check-ins, performance reviews, iteration, advisory 8–16 hrs/mo $600–$1,920/mo What We Offer • A founder who is highly involved, fast-moving, and deeply committed to the brand • A clear brand identity, finalized formulas, and a real audience already in place • Existing infrastructure: Shopify, Klaviyo, Judge.me, Meta Ads, Instagram, Reddit presence • Openness to honest strategic input — we want a real partner, not a yes person • Long-term potential: we're building this to grow How to Apply In your proposal, please include: • Examples of pet brand work specifically — links, results, or brief case studies • Which engagement type(s) interest you and your hourly rate • A brief note on how you'd approach customer/audience analysis as a starting point • Why the senior dog category speaks to you, even briefly. This brand was built on connection. A Note from the Founder ODNT was built from personal experience caring for an aging dog, and years of ingredient research that most people never do. This brand is for the people who love their senior dogs fiercely and want real answers, not filler. The right marketing partner will understand that emotional truth — and know how to build a bridge between science and love.

jobs.description: We are looking for an experienced E-commerce Customer Support Operations Manager to manage disputes, refunds, fulfillment checks, and customer service quality control across multiple Shopify dropshipping stores. This is not a basic customer support agent role. We already have customer service agents. Each agent is responsible for one store. Your role will be to manage the customer service operation above them, make sure cases are handled correctly, and take the dispute/refund/fulfillment workload away from me. The most important part of this role is disputes. You must have strong experience handling disputes, chargebacks, refund cases, Shopify Payments cases, and ideally Klarna-related cases. I need someone who can proactively monitor, manage, and update disputes instead of waiting for me to handle everything. Main responsibilities: • Manage Shopify Payments disputes and chargebacks • Prepare and submit dispute updates/evidence when needed • Handle Klarna-related customer cases, refunds, and disputes • Review refund requests from customer service agents • Process approved refunds in Shopify • Escalate only the cases that truly need my decision • Manage and quality-check customer service agents • Review customer service conversations and make sure agents follow our policies • Give feedback to agents after approval from me • Coordinate with fulfillment agents/suppliers when there are order issues • Check if orders are fulfilled on time • Investigate why orders are not fulfilled, delayed, or stuck • Check whether orders are shipped within the correct time frame • Monitor tracking, fulfillment status, and delivery issues • Communicate with the fulfillment team to solve order problems • Send me a daily update with processed refunds, pending refunds, open disputes, fulfillment issues, and customer service problems How we work: We use Slack for internal communication. Customer service agents currently tag me when there is a refund request, dispute, or customer issue. I want to change this process so that you become the main person responsible for reviewing and managing these cases. You will work in a Slack group with me, the customer service agents, and the fulfillment contact. You will be expected to proactively check disputes, refunds, and fulfillment issues instead of waiting for me to chase everything. Required experience: • Shopify experience • Shopify Payments dispute or chargeback experience • Klarna experience is strongly preferred • E-commerce customer service experience • Experience managing or supervising customer service agents • Experience with refunds, replacements, order issues, and fulfillment problems • Experience using Slack or similar tools • Strong English communication • Good judgment and attention to detail The ideal person: You are organized, proactive, and experienced. You understand that the goal is not just to answer customers, but to protect the business, reduce unnecessary refunds, handle disputes correctly, and make sure the customer service team is working properly. You should be able to take ownership of the customer service operation, identify problems before they become bigger, and give clear daily updates. Please do not apply if you only have basic customer support experience. I am looking for someone who has managed customer service operations and has real experience with disputes, chargebacks, refunds, Shopify, Klarna, and fulfillment checks. To apply, please answer these questions: What experience do you have with Shopify Payments disputes or chargebacks? What experience do you have with Klarna? Have you managed or quality-checked customer service agents before?

jobs.description: Company: Puresport Puresport is one of the UK's leading wellness brands, renowned for creating innovative, natural products that set new industry standards. Our mission is to revolutionise the wellness industry with solutions that empower people to live healthier lives. We are dedicated to product excellence, sustainability, and enhancing our customers' well-being. Join a team shaping the future of wellness with creativity, purpose, and passion. Department: Operations Reports to: Head of Operations Location: Remote (UK Timings) Role Overview: Puresport is looking for a highly organised Logistics Specialist to support the Head of Operations in managing and improving the company’s downstream operational activity. This role will be responsible for overseeing the movement of goods from suppliers through to warehouse, retail customers and international markets. The successful candidate will manage key operational workflows including supplier-to-warehouse transportation, inbound shipments from China to the UK, warehouse performance, retail order fulfilment, logistics coordination, stock visibility and process improvement. The role requires someone who can bring structure, control and professionalism into the operation, while improving efficiency, visibility, accountability and service levels across the supply chain. Key Responsibilities:- 1. Supplier to Warehouse Transportation Manage the movement of goods from suppliers and manufacturers into Puresport’s warehouse network. Responsibilities include: • Coordinating inbound deliveries from suppliers to warehouses. • Ensuring all shipments are booked, tracked and delivered on time. • Managing communication between suppliers, freight partners, warehouses and internal teams. • Identifying and resolving delays, delivery issues or documentation gaps. • Ensuring inbound delivery schedules are aligned with stock requirements and commercial priorities. • Maintaining visibility of goods in transit and expected warehouse receipt dates. 2. International Shipment Management Oversee international logistics, particularly shipments from China to the UK. Responsibilities include: • Managing international freight movements by sea, air or courier as required. • Coordinating with freight forwarders, suppliers and customs agents. • Monitoring shipment timelines, port activity, customs clearance and final delivery. • Supporting landed cost visibility and helping improve freight planning. • Ensuring the business has clear visibility of international inbound stock. 3. Warehouse Management and 3PL Performance Manage day-to-day operational relationships with warehouse and fulfilment partners. Responsibilities include: • Acting as the key operational contact for warehouse and 3PL partners. • Monitoring warehouse performance across inbound receiving, storage, picking, packing, dispatch and returns. • Ensuring warehouse SLAs are clearly defined, tracked and met. • Managing stock accuracy and discrepancy resolution. • Reviewing fulfilment performance, cost, service levels and operational issues. • Ensuring warehouse processes are scalable and suitable for future business growth. 4. Retail Order Management Manage the operational execution of retail, wholesale and B2B orders. Responsibilities include: • Coordinating retail order fulfilment from order receipt through to dispatch and delivery. • Ensuring retailer-specific requirements are met, including delivery windows, labelling, packaging, documentation and routing requirements. • Managing communication with internal sales, customer service, warehouse and logistics teams. • Tracking retail order status and proactively escalating risks. • Supporting OTIF performance - on time, in full delivery. • Helping create a more structured and professional retail order management process. 5. Data Visibility and Operational Reporting Improve visibility across the downstream supply chain so the business has accurate, timely and useful operational data. Responsibilities include: • Building and maintaining clear trackers for inbound shipments, warehouse stock, retail orders and logistics performance. • Improving data accuracy across systems, spreadsheets and operational reports. • Creating regular reporting for the Head of Operations and wider business. • Tracking key metrics such as inbound delivery status, warehouse performance, stock accuracy, fulfilment performance, freight costs and retail order status. 6. Process Improvement and Operational Efficiency Bring structure, discipline and continuous improvement into Puresport’s downstream operations. Responsibilities include: • Reviewing existing operational processes and identifying inefficiencies. • Creating clear SOPs, process maps and working documents. • Introducing better controls around inbound logistics, warehouse management, retail fulfilment and stock movement. • Reducing manual work, duplicated effort and avoidable operational issues. • Helping professionalise the operations function as the business scales. Required Skills and Experience The ideal candidate will have: • Experience in supply chain, logistics, warehousing, fulfilment or operations management. • Strong understanding of inbound logistics, warehouse operations and order fulfilment. • Experience managing freight forwarders, 3PLs, warehouses or logistics partners. • Knowledge of international shipping, ideally including China-to-UK import processes. • Experience handling retail, wholesale or B2B orders. • Strong spreadsheet, tracking and reporting skills. • Excellent organisation and attention to detail. • Ability to manage multiple moving parts and prioritise effectively. • Strong communication skills with both internal teams and external partners. • A proactive, problem-solving mindset. • Ability to create structure where processes are unclear or underdeveloped. • Comfort working in a fast-moving, scaling business. Desirable Experience The following would be beneficial but not essential: • Experience in FMCG, wellness, supplements, nutrition, beauty, consumer goods or ecommerce. • Experience working with Shopify, ERP, WMS, inventory management or order management systems. • Knowledge of customs documentation, Incoterms and landed cost calculations. • Experience improving warehouse SLAs and fulfilment KPIs. • Experience supporting retail accounts or major retailer delivery requirements. • Experience implementing SOPs, dashboards or operational reporting tools. This role would suit someone who is highly organised, detail-oriented and comfortable taking ownership of operational complexity. They should be hands-on enough to manage day-to-day logistics issues, but structured enough to build better processes, reporting and controls. The ideal person will not simply “coordinate tasks” they will bring discipline, visibility and professionalism into the operation, helping Puresport scale with stronger systems, clearer accountability and more reliable execution.
jobs.title: Logistics Specialist

jobs.description: Stack: vanilla JS + Liquid, no frameworks. Hands-on commercial experience with Theme App Extensions and AJAX Cart API is mandatory - this is platform-specific knowledge that general JS experience cannot substitute. DOM and checkout flow issues. We are seeking an experienced Shopify JavaScript developer to work on a project involving the DOM API (cart.js,etc.) . The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of modern JavaScript features such as async/await, Promise, fetch, destructuring, and arrow functions. Experience with DOM API methods like MutationObserver, querySelector, closest, and insertBefore is essential. The role involves developing and maintaining web applications, ensuring high performance and responsiveness. The script embeds hardcoded CSS selectors for Dawn and Horizon themes directly inside its logic — selectors for the cart drawer, cart page, total price element, checkout button, and quantity inputs. These are scattered across 5 different places in the code, mixed in with business logic. Every time a merchant uses a different theme (Prestige, Sense, Debut, etc.), the script either silently fails to insert the widget or inserts it in the wrong place — and the fix requires a developer to open the theme in DevTools, find the right selectors, and patch the script manually. The goal of this refactor is to extract all selector dependencies into a single configuration layer at the top of the file, so that adding theme support becomes a config change rather than a code change.
jobs.title: Shopify JS Expert for Widget development all plans

jobs.description: # Ice District Co. — Shopify Store Setup (Full Service) ## Project Overview I’m launching **Ice District Co.**, a new direct-to-consumer jewelry brand selling necklaces, rings, handle the complete build, end-to-end, and hand me a finished, working store. ## Required Qualifications — Please Do Not Apply Unless You Meet These * **Verified Shopify Partner status** (link to your Shopify Partners profile in your bid) * **Minimum 3 live Shopify stores** in your portfolio that I can click through and review (jewelry, fashion, or accessories preferred) * **Job Success Score of 95%+ on Upwork** with at least 20 completed projects * **Top Rated** or **Top Rated Plus** badge * Available to begin within the next week * Fluent written English for clear communication Bids that don’t address all six items above will be ignored. ## What I Will Provide * Logo file (PNG/SVG) * Product photos for each item (image files only — you do not need to take photos) * Product info: names, prices, descriptions, materials, sizes/variants * Brand colors and style preferences * Business info for legal pages and payment processor signup * Custom domain name ## Scope of Work ### Store Setup * Configure Shopify account on the appropriate plan and complete all backend settings * Install and customize a premium Shopify theme appropriate for a jewelry brand (recommend in your proposal — I’ll cover the theme cost separately, $200–400 typical) * Apply logo and brand colors throughout the site * Connect custom domain with SSL/HTTPS ### Pre-Launch Landing Page (Live During Store Build) While the full store is being developed, create and launch a branded pre-launch landing page on my domain to collect customer signups before launch. * Build a professional branded “Coming Soon” page matching the store’s design * Include signup form with: * Email address (required) * Phone number (optional) * Store all signup information in an organized customer list * Connect signup data to [Klaviyo](https://www.klaviyo.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com) or [Shopify Email](https://www.shopify.com/email-marketing?utm_source=chatgpt.com) * Segment contacts for launch notifications * Allow export of signup list (CSV) * Countdown timer to launch date * Ensure pre-launch page stays active while main store is under development ### Product & Category Setup * Create all product categories (necklaces, rings, watches, bracelets, expandable) * Upload all product photos and create every product listing with descriptions, prices, variants, and inventory counts * Set up navigation menus and collection pages * Build a **jewelry sizing guide page** with visual charts (ring sizes, necklace lengths, bracelet measurements) * Configure trust badges near the buy button ### Payments & Checkout * Configure **[Shopify Payments](https://www.shopify.com/payments?utm_source=chatgpt.com)** so customers can pay with Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover * Enable Apple Pay, Google Pay, and PayPal as additional checkout options * Enable optional gift wrapping at checkout with a gift message field * Set up tax calculation and shipping rates (zones and rates provided) ### Returns Management * Configure Shopify’s built-in returns workflow: customer-initiated requests, owner approval in admin, prepaid return labels, refunds * Build a clear Returns & Exchanges policy page (policy details provided) * Confirm **[Shopify Protect](https://www.shopify.com/protect?utm_source=chatgpt.com)** is enabled ### Customer Reviews * Install and configure [Judge.me](https://judge.me/?utm_source=chatgpt.com) (or [Loox](https://loox.io/?utm_source=chatgpt.com) if photo reviews recommended) with moderation enabled * Set up automated post-purchase review request emails * Display approved reviews on product pages and homepage ### Customer Features * Customer accounts: signup, login, order history * Wishlist / Save for Later app * Email newsletter signup connected to Klaviyo or Shopify Email * Abandoned cart recovery emails * Order confirmation and shipping notification emails * Back-in-stock notifications * [Shopify Inbox](https://www.shopify.com/inbox?utm_source=chatgpt.com) for live chat ### Pages & Content * Build out: Home, Shop/Collections, About, Contact, FAQ, Sizing Guide, Shipping & Returns, Privacy Policy, Terms of Service * Configure cookie consent banner for GDPR/CCPA compliance ### Technical * Install [Google Analytics 4](https://analytics.google.com/analytics/web/?utm_source=chatgpt.com) * Install [Meta Pixel](https://www.facebook.com/business/tools/meta-pixel?utm_source=chatgpt.com) * Basic SEO (meta titles, descriptions, sitemap, product structured data) * Confirm Shopify Magic and Shopify Sidekick are enabled and demonstrate them in training * Mobile-responsive and speed-optimized ## Deliverables 1. Fully launched, working Shopify store on my domain 2. Pre-launch signup page active before store launch 3. Customer signup list organized for email/SMS notifications 4. **1-hour live training session** covering: * Adding new products and updating inventory * Viewing and fulfilling orders * Processing returns and issuing refunds * Moderating customer reviews * Responding to chargeback disputes * Creating discount codes 5. Confirm your proposed timeline in your bid. ## To Apply Your bid must include: 1. Link to your Shopify Partners profile 2. Links to 3–5 live Shopify stores you’ve built 3. jobs.description: Project Overview I run an established historical-imagery e-commerce store on BigCommerce with roughly 300,000 product listings. The catalog has accumulated three problems that need a single, careful pass to fix: (1) image-directory paths that need to be renamed across all image URL fields, (2) duplicate image entries on product pages caused by a prior upload script crashing and restarting multiple times, and (3) near-identical boilerplate titles and meta/product descriptions that are tanking indexation. I already have the new shuffled SEO copy written and ready to drop in — no AI rewriting is required. This is a focused execution job. The strategy is set, the new copy is provided, and the rules are spelled out. I need someone who can work the BC v3 API confidently and run a clean, sequenced data pass. Scope of Work (5 steps, in order) Do not skip steps. Run them in the order below. Pull a fresh full product export from BigCommerce via v3 API. Do not use the 2024 feed file as the working source. We need current live data as the basis for every downstream change. Image URL directory updates. Across every image URL field (Product Image File 1 through 10), perform two find-and-replace operations: /Framed/ → /FramedNew/ and /DropShadow/ → /DropShadowNew/. Remove duplicate image entries per product. Rule: each product should have exactly one framed image, one drop shadow image, one of each room preview (Room1, Room2), and one swatch. Any URL that appears more than once across a product's image slots should be deduplicated — keep the first instance, delete every subsequent duplicate. Apply this rule across all SKUs. No row-by-row validation from me is needed; the rule is the rule. Titles and descriptions — update the BC catalog. I will provide a spreadsheet with new SKU → Product Name, Meta Description, and Product Description values. Import these against the live catalog. No rewriting on your end — just clean, accurate import. Move existing BC boilerplate description text to a non-indexed field. The repeating boilerplate that currently sits inside the indexed product description needs to be relocated to a field type that Google does not crawl (custom tab, custom field, or similar). Confirm which BC field type you plan to use before making changes. Out of Scope Pricing updates (already complete — $219 framed, $57 unframed 16×20, $71 unframed 18×24) Image regeneration or new image creation Front-end theme work Any AI/LLM rewriting of titles or descriptions — the new copy is already written Deliverables Confirmation that the fresh v3 API export was pulled, with record count Before/after sample of the image URL directory updates on 5+ SKUs Dedupe summary: total duplicates removed, broken down by product if practical Confirmation of the BC field type chosen for the boilerplate move, before execution Final import confirmation for titles, meta descriptions, and product descriptions Short end-of-job note summarizing what was changed and any anomalies encountered Budget & Timeline Type: Fixed-price Budget: $500 USD Target start: Within 48 hours of contract acceptance Target completion: Within 10–14 days of start, sequenced as above Milestones: 1/2 up front, 1/2 upon completion - released on completion and verification of all 5 scope items Skills & Experience Required Hands-on experience with the BigCommerce v3 API (catalog endpoints, product import/export) Comfort with large catalog data work — 100K+ SKU range Bulk find-and-replace and deduplication on tabular product data Familiarity with BC field types and which are indexed vs. non-indexed Clean, sequenced execution — willingness to follow a 6-step order without skipping or reordering
jobs.title: BigCommerce Catalog Cleanup, Image Dedupe, and SEO Title/Description Update (v3 API)

jobs.description: About the store spoutwater.com — premium single-product Shopify store. $1,495 atmospheric water generator. Currently on Shopify Advanced. ~80% mobile traffic, ~35% from FB/IG in-app browser environments. Making sales daily. I have a cluster of related issues that need a senior Shopify dev with depth in tracking, performance, and checkout. I also have a new theme version on a preview link that needs pre-launch QA before publishing over the live site. Scope (prioritized) 1. Tracking integrity (most important) Purchase event firing ~1x/week in Clarity while we're making daily sales. Clarity is losing users when checkout moves to checkout.shopify.com. AddToCart count lower than Begin Checkout — Shop Pay / dynamic checkout buttons appear to be bypassing the cart event. Need GA4, Meta Pixel, and Clarity firing consistent Purchase/AddToCart/Begin Checkout events across both cart-led and Shop Pay paths. Strongly prefer server-side via GTM + Stape (or equivalent) for iOS 14 + ad-blocker resilience. 2. PDP performance LCP 6.844s on Clarity, 45.4s on PageSpeed mobile. PageSpeed Performance score 28. PageSpeed flags: render-blocking resources, unused JS, large payload, long main-thread tasks. Target: LCP under 2.5s mobile, performance score 70+. 3. In-app browser handling (FB/IG) ~35% of traffic from FB/IG in-app browsers. Error log shows window.webkit.messagehandlers undefined errors in 46 sessions (iOS in-app fingerprint). Need either (a) detection + "Open in Safari/Chrome" handoff pattern, or (b) hardened in-app checkout experience. Want your recommendation on which. 4. Smaller fixes Affirm publicKey duplicate firing Source maps missing (57% of JS errors return generic "script error.") Persistent button overlapping the review submit button on PDP — need clean repro and fix Quick console error sweep 5. Pre-launch QA on preview theme I'll share a Shopify preview link. Want a delta analysis: what's better, what's worse, what's net-new broken vs. the live site. Context that helps I've already done significant Clarity-driven debugging (Slick carousel double-init, cart drawer hang from missing Accept: application/json header, broken AddToCart tracking). I know my way around the technical surface — you won't need to over-explain. I just need a senior pair of hands faster than mine. Timeline & budget Budget: ~$1,500–$2,000. Open to hourly or fixed-price. Hourly preferred — I'd rather pay for your real time than have you pad against scope risk. Timeline: ideally 24–72 hours start to finish. If the full scope is too tight for that window, I'm open to a Phase 1 (tracking + critical bugs, immediate) and Phase 2 (performance + in-app browser, this week). What I value Substantive, direct communication. Quick replies. Willingness to push back if my framing is wrong. Honest scope estimates. No padding.

jobs.description: # 3D Trailer Configurator Developer (WebGL / Three.js / GLB) and Business Workflow/ Automation We are looking for an experienced 3D/WebGL developer or small team to create a premium interactive online trailer configurator for a trailer manufacturing business. The project involves: 1. Creating high-quality, realistic 3D trailer models 2. Optimising the models for real-time browser performance 3. Developing the full online 3D configurator 4. Integrating quote/invoice generation functionality 5. Producing production build summaries/instructions 6. Creating backend workflow and quote/order tracking functionality The overall goal is to create a highly visual, conversion-focused online sales tool that also functions as an internal business workflow platform. --- # Scope of Project The configurator will include approximately: * 25–30 trailer models/variants * Multiple size variations * Approx. 15 configurable options/features Examples may include: * Cage options * Wheel/rim selections * Toolboxes * Ramps * Mudguards * Finishes/colours * Lighting/accessories * Braking/suspension options The system should visually update in real time as options are selected, while dynamically updating pricing. --- # 3D Modelling Requirements We require premium-looking but lightweight real-time models suitable for browser-based rendering. Models should: * Be optimised for real-time web use (not ultra-heavy architectural rendering) * Use clean topology and efficient geometry * Be modular/component-based for interchangeable options * Include proper UV mapping * Use PBR materials/textures * Be exportable in GLB/glTF format * Be suitable for Three.js/WebGL workflows * Be lightweight enough for fast loading on desktop and mobile Preferred geometry target: * Approx. 40k–60k triangles per trailer where practical The visual outcome should feel premium, realistic and automotive-inspired while remaining highly responsive online. --- # Configurator Requirements The configurator should: * Run smoothly in-browser * Work across desktop and mobile * Allow users to visually customise trailers in real time * Dynamically update pricing * Allow users to: * Submit enquiries * Generate quotes * Reserve/order trailers * Potentially proceed to online purchase later The configurator should be designed with scalability in mind for future expansion. --- # Business Workflow & Automation Requirements The platform should not only function as a visual configurator, but also as an internal workflow and sales management system. Based on customer selections, the system should be capable of: * Generating printable PDF quotes * Generating invoices * Logging customer enquiries and quote data into a database/CRM * Tracking quote status and sales pipeline * Tracking order progress and production timelines * Producing production-ready build sheets/instructions * Producing itemised specifications for workshop/manufacturing staff * Recording selected options, dimensions and configurations * Creating internal job/project records * Potentially integrating with accounting or ERP systems in future The intention is to streamline the entire process from: Customer Enquiry → Configuration → Quote → Sale → Production → Delivery --- # Preferred Technologies Open to recommendations, however likely technologies may include: * Three.js * WebGL * React / React Three Fiber * Node.js * Database-driven backend * GLB/glTF workflows Please advise your recommended tech stack and workflow. --- # Ideal Applicant We are specifically looking for developers or teams with experience in: * WebGL / Three.js configurators * Real-time 3D product visualisation * Database-driven web applications * Quoting/order management systems * Workflow automation * eCommerce or manufacturing systems * CRM/backend integrations * PDF/document generation * Automotive or mechanical product visualisation preferred --- # Application Requirements Please include: 1. Portfolio/examples of relevant configurator or WebGL work 2. Examples of GLB/glTF assets 3. Your recommended workflow/tech stack 4. Confirmation you can handle BOTH: - modelling - configurator development 5. Precise pricing to create one trailer model for initial evaluation 6. Precise pricing structure and timeline for entire project 7. Whether you are an individual or team --- # Additional Notes This project may initially begin as a proof-of-concept using a smaller number of trailer models before expanding into the full product range. We are looking for a long-term development partner and ongoing work may be available for the right developer/team.

jobs.description: Project Overview: We are building a mobile e-commerce app using Shopify as the backend. Our product data is imported through CSV files from sources such as Amazon, Shein, Alibaba, and similar platforms. The CSV contains raw category paths in the Type column, while the Product Category column is blank. Shopify requires the Product Category field to exactly match Shopify’s official Standard Product Taxonomy, otherwise the import may fail. We need a specialist to create a reliable category-mapping system. Scope of Work: * Review an initial sample CSV of around 1,000 products * Map raw source categories to Shopify’s official Standard Product Taxonomy * Fill the Product Category column with valid Shopify categories * Leave the field blank only when no reasonable Shopify equivalent exists * Deliver an import-ready CSV * Provide a reusable mapping file or script for future batches * Document the process clearly so we can reuse it internally Required Skills: * Strong experience with Shopify CSV imports * Familiarity with Shopify Standard Product Taxonomy * Data processing experience using Python, Excel, Google Sheets, or Power Query * Experience with Amazon, Shein, Alibaba, or marketplace category structures * High attention to detail * English communication required; Arabic is a bonus Deliverables: * Final cleaned CSV with Shopify Product Category populated * Reusable mapping file/script * Short documentation explaining the logic * Optional Loom/video walkthrough Project Type: Fixed-price or hourly Experience Level: Intermediate to Expert Estimated Duration: 1–2 weeks, with potential ongoing work
